commits.subject: test(0.8.3/Slice 5 fix-1): RED — >=2-session class predicate + runtime validate_repin raise (codex §9 2xP1+P2)
fix(0.8.3/Slice 5 fix-1): enforce ≥2-session class predicate + isolate Mem0 collection + wire validate_repin at runtime (codex §9 2×P1+P2)

findings_addressed.fix: Added corpus_validity.class_predicate_ok(): multi_session & knowledge_update REQUIRE evidence resolving (via session_id_of) to >=2 distinct sessions; factoid/temporal keep >=1-evidence. Enforced in 3 places: augment_class() rejects non-conforming generations; a build-time filter pass in build_repin(); and the runtime validate_repin() guard. knowledge_update targeted prompt (gold_gen.py) now demands >=2 doc_ids (earlier + superseding session). Gold REGENERATED offline ($0 local qwen3.6-27b): zero single-session span-class cases (d0a-0441/d0a-0235 taint gone); all 4 classes reach n_min=150. NO class hit the HITL under-fill escalation.

Chroma collection name AND on-disk path now keyed by corpus_hash + a per-run run_id (minted fresh when omitted); user_id derived per-run via run_user_id(run_id) (no longer fixed). A re-run cannot reopen a prior run's collection / read stale memories — reproducible by construction, no manual /tmp/mem0_chroma wipe. Local-only (no cloud, ADR §3.6).

load_repin_gold() now calls corpus_validity.validate_repin() over the gold (per-class counts derived from the file, n_min from the file) and RAISES ValueError on any problem (N=0 / under-n_min / missing class / single-session-mislabeled span class). from_repin_gold() routes through load_repin_gold(), so it raises too. RED test test_from_repin_gold_raises_on_underfilled_manifest asserts the raise.

new_corpus_hash.corpus_hash_note: INVARIANT vs pre-fix (1859817a): the LME corpus (documents) is deterministic and did not change — only the gold/qrels changed. An honest corpus_hash cannot change; faking one would misrepresent the corpus. LOUD JUSTIFIED DEVIATION from the literal 'new corpus_hash' ask.
new_corpus_hash.identity_change: The fix-1 gold change is captured by the NEW qrels_hash (sha256 over emitted gold) and repin_hash = sha256(corpus_hash || qrels_hash). repin_hash (2916cace, was effectively repin-1859817a) is the changing identity downstream keys off.

hitl_escalation.detail: All four classes reached n_min=150 AFTER the >=2-session filter. knowledge_update (78 LME-real, all already >=2-session) topped up with 72 valid >=2-session synthetic via local qwen3.6-27b (strengthened KU prompt -> high valid yield, reached 72/72 in 13 batches of 38 available); multi_session +17, temporal +17. No under-filled class shipped; no predicate relaxed to hit counts.

blockers_encountered.detail: The .venv editable install resolves `import eval` to the CANONICAL repo's src/python, NOT this worktree (the agent-worktree stale-base trap). The first regen run silently executed UNMODIFIED canonical gold_repin.py and produced stale-format artifacts. Resolved by running the builder AND pytest with PYTHONPATH=<worktree>/src/python prepended so the worktree's edited modules win. No native build was run (FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1). After the orchestrator cherry-picks these commits into canonical, the prompt's self-check command runs clean WITHOUT the PYTHONPATH override.

self_check.pytest_selection: 38 passed, 320 deselected (FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1, PYTHONPATH=worktree/src/python, -k 'd0a or corpus_valid or mem0 or r2 or repin or class')
self_check.python_guard: GUARD PASS: every class >= n_min {factoid:156, knowledge_update:150, multi_session:150, temporal:150}; ZERO single-session multi_session/knowledge_update cases
self_check.canonical_repo_status: no MY changes leaked; only pre-existing untracked orchestrator files (review .md/.log + steward handoff) present

doc_index_note: No new file added (only edited existing eval modules + the namespaced design doc); DOC-INDEX left unchanged per instruction.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Cherry-pick RED 02b8726b + GREEN 88ba3772 onto main; re-run codex §9 review on the fix-1 net diff; then close Slice 5. Note the LOUD justified deviation: corpus_hash is intentionally INVARIANT (1859817a) because the LME corpus is deterministic/unchanged — the new identity is qrels_hash/repin_hash (2916cace) / qrels_version repin-2916cace. The re-review should accept repin_hash as the changing identity rather than expecting a faked corpus_hash. After cherry-pick the prompt's self-check runs clean without the PYTHONPATH override (editable install then resolves eval to canonical = the fixed code).
